title: ds-viz-markergauge - option matrix
description: Test bench for splunk.markergauge. The 10.4 reference exposes 7 options total: backgroundColor, gaugeRanges, labelDisplay, majorTickInterval, orientation, value, valueDisplay. This dashboard walks each one with vertical and horizontal layouts and threshold patterns.

options.markdown:
# `splunk.markergauge` - option matrix

A single value plotted as a marker against a banded scale. Use it for KPIs that have a *target zone* — NPS scores, CSAT, latency budgets, capacity bands. The colour comes from `gaugeRanges`; the marker shows where the value sits *within* those bands. Different from `splunk.singlevalueradial` (which fills `value/maxValue` with a single colour) and from `splunk.fillergauge` (which fills the band itself).

Only 7 options exist on this viz. **Default orientation is vertical.**

viz_default_low.title: 1. Default - vertical, low value (22)
viz_default_low.description: Pure default. orientation=vertical, labelDisplay=number, valueDisplay=number, gaugeRanges falls back to a 3-band default. The marker sits at the bottom of the gauge - low value reads instantly.

viz_default_mid.title: 2. Default - vertical, mid value (58)
viz_default_mid.description: Same options, mid value. The marker sits in the middle band. The default colour palette is theme-driven.

viz_default_high.title: 3. Default - vertical, high value (94)
viz_default_high.description: Same options, high value. Marker sits near the top.

viz_horizontal.title: 4. orientation: horizontal
viz_horizontal.description: Same data, horizontal axis. Use horizontal in dense rows of KPIs where vertical gauges would cramp; vertical reads better when the gauge has space to breathe (executive boards).

viz_traffic_light.title: 5. gaugeRanges - traffic light (low good, high bad)
viz_traffic_light.description: Three-band traffic light using the user-confirmed syntax: `[{"from":0,"to":50,"value":"#33FF99"},{"from":50,"to":80,"value":"#FFB627"},{"from":80,"to":100,"value":"#FF2D95"}]`. Bands MUST be contiguous - leave a gap and the missing range renders grey.

viz_inverse_traffic.title: 6. gaugeRanges - inverse (high good, low bad)
viz_inverse_traffic.description: Same bands flipped. Use for metrics where high IS good (uptime, completion, NPS). The marker on the far right reads as 'in the green zone'.

viz_nps_bands.title: 7. NPS bands (-100 to 100 mapped to 0-100)
viz_nps_bands.description: NPS score banded as Detractor / Passive / Promoter. Real NPS is -100..100; here we use 0..100 with `from:0, to:30` (detractor), `from:30, to:70` (passive), `from:70, to:100` (promoter). Marker at 64 reads as 'passive, leaning promoter'.

viz_latency.title: 8. Latency budget (0-1000ms)
viz_latency.description: Bands extended to 1000. The gauge auto-scales to the gaugeRanges total span. Marker at 320ms reads as 'inside the warning zone'.

viz_csat_small.title: 9. CSAT 0-5 (small range)
viz_csat_small.description: Small ranges (0-5, 0-10) work fine. The marker at 4.2 sits in the green band. Useful for ratings, scores, grade scales.

viz_label_off.title: 10. labelDisplay: off, valueDisplay: off
viz_label_off.description: Strips the band labels and the value readout. Result is a pure visual band - useful when the dashboard already has the value in a separate singlevalue tile and the gauge is just the contextual band.

viz_label_percentage.title: 11. labelDisplay: percentage, valueDisplay: percentage
viz_label_percentage.description: Renders both band labels and the value as percentages of the total range. With `from:0, to:100` they're identical to the numbers; with `from:0, to:1000` (latency) they map to 0% / 50% / 100%. Use when the *position* matters more than the absolute number.

viz_majortick.title: 12. majorTickInterval custom + custom backgroundColor
viz_majortick.description: majorTickInterval controls the pixel spacing of the major ticks. Pin it when the auto layout produces too few or too many ticks. backgroundColor here is set to a panel-tint hex - useful for highlighting alarm tiles without losing the gauge bands.

options.markdown:
## Compact tile patterns

The Studio editor refuses to resize a `splunk.markergauge` panel below ~200x300 (vertical) or ~200x100 (horizontal). The trick to get a denser KPI bank: **set `backgroundColor: "transparent"` and place panels at the minimum size, edge-to-edge.** The panel chrome disappears and only the gauge artwork remains, so four gauges side-by-side read as one tight unit. Below: a 4-up vertical bank (panels 13-16) and a stacked horizontal bank (panels 17-20). Compare with panels 1-3 which use the default opaque chrome.

**Rules:**
- Vertical: 200x300 minimum. Use `labelDisplay: "off"` + `valueDisplay: "off"` to recover vertical space and let a sibling singlevalue carry the number.
- Horizontal: 200x100 minimum. Stack with 4-8 px gap so the gauges feel related but not glued.
- `backgroundColor: "transparent"` is the unlock. Without it you see the surface card and the trick fails.

viz_compact_v1.title: CPU
viz_compact_v1.description: Compact vertical tile - 200x300, transparent bg, value/label off. Lives inside a 4-up bank (panels 13-16). The marker plus the bands carry the message; the value would be carried by a sibling number tile.

viz_compact_v2.title: MEM
viz_compact_v2.description: Same options, mid value. Side-by-side with panel 13 the four gauges read as one unit, no inner padding.

viz_compact_v3.title: DISK
viz_compact_v3.description: Same options, high value. The marker hits the top band - red zone reads instantly.

viz_compact_v4.title: NET
viz_compact_v4.description: Fourth tile. The bank reads top-to-bottom in priority: green / amber / red. Use `labelDisplay: "off"` to make the bands themselves the eye-anchor.

viz_compact_h1.title: p50 latency
viz_compact_h1.description: Horizontal compact tile - 200x100, transparent bg. The marker on the bar shows where the value sits within the SLO bands.

viz_compact_h2.title: p95 latency
viz_compact_h2.description: Stacked under panel 17 with a 4 px gap. The vertical rhythm makes them read as a small SLO panel.

viz_compact_h3.title: p99 latency
viz_compact_h3.description: Third row in the SLO stack. Same bands - scanning vertically you see all three percentiles at once.

viz_compact_h4.title: max latency
viz_compact_h4.description: Bottom of the stack. Together panels 17-20 form a 4-row latency profile in 800x108 of vertical space - dense, but legible because the bars share a scale.
